subject: Public Health
title: Tobacco Industry Tactics
description: Learn Tobacco Industry Tactics in one minute.

hook.text: The tobacco industry wrote the playbook for corporate deception—deny, delay, and doubt the science.

buildup.text: Internal documents revealed decades of suppressed research—companies knew smoking caused cancer but hid the evidence.

discovery.text: Industry tactics included funding fake research, lobbying against regulation, and marketing to children and minorities.

twist.text: After losing in rich countries, tobacco companies shifted marketing to low- and middle-income nations.

climax.text: Exposing these tactics through litigation and leaked documents transformed public health policy worldwide.

punchline.text: The tobacco industry didn't just sell cigarettes. It sold doubt.

quiz.question: Where have tobacco companies shifted marketing as rich-country markets shrink?
